# A7 Full Context

This is the single-file context document for A7. It is meant to answer the
common project overview, usage, implementation status, known gaps, and edit
map without requiring link chasing.

For routing only, use `/a7-py/llms.txt`. For the complete Markdown document
tree, use `/a7-py/docs/index.md`.

## Project

A7 is a statically typed systems language compiler implemented in Python. It
compiles A7 source to Zig, keeps compiler stages visible, and verifies
examples through compile/build/run/output gates.

The public site is `https://code5717.github.io/a7-py/`.
The source repository is `https://github.com/code5717/a7-py`.

A7 is not a sandbox. Do not compile or run untrusted A7 source.

## Pipeline Diagram (ASCII)

```text
+--------+    +-----------+    +--------+    +----------+    +--------+    +-----------------+    +---------+    +-----+
| Source | -> | Tokenizer | -> | Parser | -> | Semantic | -> | Safety | -> | AST Preprocess  | -> | Codegen | -> | Zig |
|  .a7   |    |           |    |        |    | Analysis |    | Proof  |    |                 |    |         |    |     |
+--------+    +-----------+    +--------+    +----------+    +--------+    +-----------------+    +---------+    +-----+
```

## Architecture Diagram (ASCII)

```text
                +-------------------+
                |     a7/cli.py     |
                +---------+---------+
                          |
            +-------------+-------------+
            |                           |
   +--------v--------+         +--------v--------+
   |  a7/tokens.py   |         | a7/parser.py    |
   +--------+--------+         +--------+--------+
            |                           |
            +-------------+-------------+
                          |
                   +------v-------+
                   |  a7/passes/  | (name_resolution, type_checker, semantic_validator)
                   +------+-------+
                          |
                   +------v-------+
                   |  a7/safety.py |
                   +------+-------+
                          |
                +---------v---------+
                | a7/ast_preprocessor.py |
                +---------+---------+
                          |
                +---------v---------+
                | a7/backends/zig.py |
                +-------------------+
```

## Current Status

Implemented:

- Tokenizer, parser, semantic pipeline, internal safety proof planning, AST preprocessing, and Zig code generation.
- CLI entrypoints through `uv run a7` and `uv run python main.py`.
- Static typing with inference in supported contexts.
- Functions, structs, enums, untagged union field literals/access, aliases, virtual stdlib modules, and generics.
- Simple top-level generic function calls, type-set constraints, and used generic struct instances in Zig.
- `if`, `while`, `for`, `for-in`, `match`, labeled loops, `break`, and `continue`.
- `fall` in match statements, restricted to the final direct statement of a non-final case.
- Arrays, slices, string slices, `slice.ptr` and `slice.len` access, string sub-slicing (`string[start..end]` and `string[start..]` as `[]char`), references, implicit ref-argument passing, and manual `new` / `del`.
- Same-shape numeric fixed arrays support element-wise `+` assignment in Zig.
- `defer`, unreachable-statement diagnostics, and direct/mutual/alias/callback-trampoline source-recursion diagnostics.
- Match identifier capture patterns bind the scrutinee in branch-local scope when no visible symbol exists with that name.
- `usize` index variables, invalid-comparison rejection, and safer signed/unsigned assignment checks.
- Internal facts and backend-plan approval for casts, division/modulo,
  indexing/slicing, and reference dereferences before Zig emission.
- Buffered Zig stdio lowering through shared `writerStreaming` stdout/stderr writers, generated print helpers, and deferred per-stream flushing from `main`.
- C backend support has been retired. Zig is the only supported code generation target; CI, release, and example verification gates focus on Zig output.
- Virtual stdlib imports for `std/io`, `io`, `std/math`, and `math`.
- 43 runnable examples verified through the Zig backend.
- Debug/release artifact verification for Zig.
- Tag-based draft GitHub releases with package, docs, and native example artifacts.

Known gaps:

- Advanced match diagnostics catch shared-endpoint symbolic range overlaps, but
  do not infer arbitrary symbolic inequalities.
- Ownership, borrowing, lifetime, use-after-free, and double-free guarantees are not implemented.
- Heap fixed arrays (`new [N]T`) are rejected until representation is defined.
- File-backed selected imports parse as metadata but do not introduce direct
  unqualified names for runnable backend code; `using import` is planned syntax,
  not a current parser form.
- Full generic specialization is incomplete beyond simple top-level generic functions and used generic struct instances.
- Variadic declarations are parsed and partially type-checked in semantic mode,
  but runtime iteration and backend ABI lowering are not implemented. Codegen
  modes reject them before target emission.
- Intrinsics beyond `@type_set(...)` are reserved/tokenized but not current
  semantic or backend features.
- Multiple return values / destructuring are planned syntax, not current parser support.
- Tagged/discriminated union tag workflows are not implemented.
- `std/string`, `std/mem`, and collections are planned but not current public stdlib modules.
- Package-registry publishing is not part of the current release workflow.

## Install

Requirements:

- Python 3.13+
- `uv`
- Zig 0.15.2 to build and run generated Zig output

Setup:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/code5717/a7-py.git
cd a7-py
uv sync
```

Check tools:

```bash
uv run a7 --help
zig version
```

## CLI

Entrypoints:

```bash
uv run a7 [OPTIONS] <file.a7>
uv run python main.py [OPTIONS] <file.a7>
```

Common commands:

```bash
uv run a7 examples/001_hello.a7
uv run a7 --mode tokens examples/006_if.a7
uv run a7 --mode ast examples/004_func.a7
uv run a7 --mode semantic --format json examples/001_hello.a7
uv run a7 --mode doc --doc-out out.md examples/001_hello.a7
```

Modes:

- `compile`: full pipeline and code generation
- `tokens`: tokenize only
- `ast`: tokenize and parse
- `semantic`: tokenize, parse, and check
- `pipeline`: full pipeline with intermediate output
- `doc`: generate a Markdown report

Flags:

- `--verbose`: show intermediate results
- `--mode MODE`: select compiler stage
- `--format json`: emit structured JSON
- `--backend zig`: select target backend (Zig is the only supported backend; C backend has been retired)
- `--doc-out PATH`: write Markdown report

## Errors and Exit Codes

| Code | Stage | Meaning |
| ---: | --- | --- |
| 0 | runtime | success |
| 2 | cli | bad arguments |
| 3 | cli | input file not found |
| 4 | tokenizer | tokenizer error |
| 5 | parser | parse error |
| 6 | semantic | semantic error |
| 7 | codegen | codegen error |
| 8 | internal | internal bug |

```text
0 success
2 bad arguments
3 file not found
4 tokenizer error
5 parse error
6 semantic error
7 codegen error
8 internal bug
```

## Language Reference

Source recursion is banned. Direct, mutual, local alias, and callback-trampoline recursion are semantic errors.
Use loops, explicit stacks, queues, or index-based worklists.

Compiler traversal note: semantic analysis, preprocessing, generic lowering,
JSON AST output, formatter/reporting walks, and backend binary-expression
emission use explicit stacks. Parser and remaining backend statement/non-binary
expression emission still have recursive paths.

Declaration forms:

```a7
CONSTANT :: 42
value := 1
typed: i32 = 1
uninitialized: i32
Alias :: i32
io :: import "std/io"
```

Function forms:

```a7
add :: fn(a: i32, b: i32) i32 {
    ret a + b
}

Callback :: fn(i32) i32
```

Composite type forms:

```a7
Point :: struct { x: i32, y: i32 }
Mode :: enum { Fast, Safe }
Value :: union { int_val: i32, flag_val: bool }
Vector :: [4]f32
SliceAlias :: []i32
```

Control-flow forms:

```a7
if ready {
    run()
} else {
    wait()
}

for i := 0; i < 10; i += 1 {
    if i == 5 { continue }
}

for value in values {
    use(value)
}

for index, value in values {
    use_index(index)
    use(value)
}

@outer while true {
    break outer
}

match mode {
    case Mode.Fast: { run_fast() }
    case Mode.Safe: { run_safe() }
}
```

Memory and pointer forms:

```a7
x: i32 = 42
bump :: fn(value: ref i32) {
    value += 1
}
bump(x)

HeapBox :: struct {
    value: i32
}

heap_value := new HeapBox
if heap_value == nil {
    ret
}
defer del heap_value
heap_value.value = 99
```

Integer guidance:

- Use `usize` for sizes, lengths, capacities, allocation byte counts, and array/slice/string indices.
- Use `isize` for signed pointer-sized offsets or differences between positions.
- Use fixed-width integers such as `i32`, `i64`, `u32`, or `u64` when the data itself has a fixed width or range.
- Small arithmetic examples can use `i32`; indexes and counters should usually use `usize`.

Current virtual stdlib:

- `std/io`: `println`, `print`, `eprintln`
- `std/math`: typed math helpers such as `sqrt_f32`, `sqrt_f64`, `abs_f32`, `abs_f64`, `min_f64`, and `max_f64`

Virtual stdlib modules are registered through the module resolver, so aliases
such as `console :: import "std/io"` and `mathlib :: import "std/math"` lower
the same way as `io` and `math`.

File-backed local imports can be resolved during semantic validation, but
Zig backend lowering and linking for multiple `.a7` files is not implemented
yet. Compile, pipeline, and doc modes reject file-backed imports before codegen.

Selected import metadata serializes in JSON diagnostics, but selected imports
do not currently introduce direct unqualified names for backend-runnable code.
`using import` remains planned syntax.

Untagged unions:

- Construct with exactly one named field: `Value{int_val: 42}`.
- Access declared fields directly: `value.int_val`.
- Tagged/discriminated workflows are not implemented yet.

## Learn The Language

Use `examples/037_language_tour.a7` as the compact learn-by-reading file. It
is a single commented program verified through Zig.

Run it:

```bash
uv run a7 examples/037_language_tour.a7
```

The interactive Language page is the Zig-style one-page reference. It covers
syntax, literals, declarations, expressions, control flow, functions, memory,
generics, modules, stdlib, and current implementation notes.

Source reference: `docs/SPEC.md`.

## Examples

The repository contains 43 runnable example programs under `examples/`.

Verifier commands:

```bash
uv run python scripts/verify_examples_e2e.py
```

The verifier compiles, builds, runs, and compares output against
`test/fixtures/golden_outputs/*.out`.

Full catalog:

- `000_empty.a7`: empty program scaffolding
- `001_hello.a7`: stdlib IO hello world
- `002_var.a7`: variables and constants
- `003_comments.a7`: comment forms
- `004_func.a7`: function declarations and calls
- `005_for_loop.a7`: classic for loop forms
- `006_if.a7`: if and else conditionals
- `007_while.a7`: while loop forms
- `008_switch.a7`: match statements (switch-style)
- `009_struct.a7`: struct declarations and field access
- `010_enum.a7`: enum declarations and matching
- `011_memory.a7`: memory management basics
- `012_arrays.a7`: fixed arrays and slices
- `013_pointers.a7`: ref parameter passing
- `014_generics.a7`: generic functions and generic struct instances
- `015_types.a7`: primitive type usage and casts
- `016_unions.a7`: current untagged union support
- `017_methods.a7`: method-style behavior using ref parameters
- `018_modules.a7`: virtual stdlib module imports
- `019_literals.a7`: literal forms across types
- `020_operators.a7`: operator surface and precedence
- `021_control_flow.a7`: loops and conditionals
- `022_function_pointers.a7`: function aliases and callbacks
- `023_inline_structs.a7`: inline struct return values
- `024_defer.a7`: defer statement behavior
- `025_linked_list.a7`: linked-list traversal with next indexes
- `026_binary_tree.a7`: explicit stack tree traversal
- `027_callbacks.a7`: callback-style event handling
- `028_state_machine.a7`: match-driven state machine
- `029_sorting.a7`: sort with comparator callback
- `030_calculator.a7`: simple calculator
- `031_number_guessing.a7`: deterministic number guessing
- `032_prime_numbers.a7`: prime checks and GCD
- `033_fibonacci.a7`: iterative Fibonacci sequence
- `034_string_utils.a7`: string utility examples
- `035_matrix.a7`: matrix operations over flat arrays
- `036_control_flow_edges.a7`: labeled loops, sub-slicing, defer scope
- `037_language_tour.a7`: commented language tour
- `038_inventory_report.a7`: inventory valuation with ref updates
- `039_text_analyzer.a7`: string slicing and char metrics
- `040_task_board.a7`: task status and risk reporting
- `041_route_simulation.a7`: iterative route movement
- `042_gradebook.a7`: nested score arrays, curves, and grades

## Compiler Pipeline

Pipeline:

```text
Source (.a7)
  -> Tokenizer
  -> Parser
  -> Semantic Analysis
  -> Safety Proof Planning
  -> AST Preprocessing
  -> Backend Codegen
  -> Zig output
```

Core source map:

- `a7/tokens.py`: tokenizer and token definitions
- `a7/parser.py`: parser
- `a7/ast_nodes.py`: AST node kinds and fields
- `a7/passes/name_resolution.py`: names and modules
- `a7/passes/type_checker.py`: type checking
- `a7/passes/semantic_validator.py`: semantic validation
- `a7/safety.py`: internal safety proof planning and backend-plan approval
- `a7/ast_preprocessor.py`: lowering annotations and preprocessing
- `a7/backends/zig.py`: Zig backend
- `a7/cli.py`: CLI entrypoint
- `main.py`: compatibility wrapper
- `scripts/`: verifiers, release tooling, docs checks
- `site/`: React documentation app and static Markdown docs

## Verification

Fast focused checks:

```bash
PYTHONPATH=. uv run pytest --tb=no -q
uv run python scripts/verify_examples_e2e.py
uv run python scripts/verify_error_stages.py
uv run python scripts/check_docs_style.py
```

Full local release-oriented gate:

```bash
./run_all_tests.sh
```

The full gate runs parser/tokenizer tests, semantic tests, compiler/CLI/backend
tests, Zig example verifier, selected backend smoke checks, debug and
release artifact builds, error-stage checks, docs style checks, secret scanning,
and the total pytest suite.

See `RELEASE_READINESS_REVIEW.md` and `COMPLETION_AUDIT.md` for recorded release-gate evidence.

## Release and Verification Commands

```bash
./run_all_tests.sh
rm -rf dist && uv build
uv run python scripts/verify_wheel_install.py --skip-build
uvx --from pip-audit==2.10.0 pip-audit --strict
uvx --from bandit==1.9.4 bandit -r a7 scripts main.py -q --skip B404,B603
(cd site && npm audit --omit=dev --audit-level=moderate)
uv run python scripts/build_examples.py --profile debug --backend zig --clean
uv run python scripts/build_examples.py --profile release --backend zig --clean
uv run python scripts/generate_release_manifest.py dist --output dist/SHA256SUMS
uv run python scripts/verify_release_manifest.py dist/SHA256SUMS
```

Release workflows:

- `.github/workflows/ci.yml`: tests, audits, docs build, examples, parity, artifacts, package build
- `.github/workflows/deploy-docs.yml`: GitHub Pages docs deploy
- `.github/workflows/release.yml`: release gate, package artifact build, docs/native archives, checksums, GitHub artifact attestations, and tag-based draft GitHub releases

Package-registry status: the current workflow does not publish to a package
registry.

## Agent Fetch Order

```text
1. https://code5717.github.io/a7-py/llms.txt          (compact routing)
2. https://code5717.github.io/a7-py/docs/index.md     (document tree)
3. https://code5717.github.io/a7-py/llms-full.txt     (this aggregate)
```

Use the compact index first. Use the document tree when you need a specific
page route. Use this aggregate when a single combined context file is easier
than many small fetches.

## Plugins

- Amp: `/a7-py/docs/plugins/amp.md`
- Claude Code: `/a7-py/docs/plugins/claude.md`
- Codex: `/a7-py/docs/plugins/codex.md`
- Cursor: `/a7-py/docs/plugins/cursor.md`
- OpenCode: `/a7-py/docs/plugins/opencode.md`
- Pi: `/a7-py/docs/plugins/pi.md`

## Documentation

Human-facing:

- Site: `/a7-py/`
- One-page language reference: `/a7-py/#/language`
- Examples: `/a7-py/#/examples`

Markdown:

- `/a7-py/llms.txt`: compact routing index
- `/a7-py/llms-full.txt`: this full-context document
- `/a7-py/docs/index.md`: complete Markdown document tree
- `/a7-py/docs/language.md`: language and stdlib summary
- `/a7-py/docs/examples.md`: examples and verifier commands
- `/a7-py/docs/compiler.md`: pipeline and tests
- `/a7-py/docs/status.md`: current status and known gaps
- `/a7-py/docs/release.md`: release commands and artifact status

Repo docs that should stay aligned after major changes:

- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `README.md`
- `docs/SPEC.md`
- `MISSING_FEATURES.md`
- `TODO.md`
- `site/public/llms.txt`
- `site/public/llms-full.txt`
- `site/public/docs/`

## Agent Notes

Read `AGENTS.md` before editing the repo.

When changing language behavior, examples, public docs, release tooling, or
site content, update the affected docs and run focused checks before the full
gate.

Do not generate recursive A7 source. Keep examples iterative.

Treat tests as evidence, not a substitute for source review, hosted workflow
state, and manual inspection of verifier output.
